calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Bryan Chauvel.
Dept. 514. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Francisco G. Torres, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff 100 Van Ness Associates, LLC.
Justin Sielbach, Assistant Manager for 100 Van Ness Associates LLC, was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Sielbach appeared for this morning's proceedings via CourtCall.
No appearance by defendant Bryan Chauvel.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Trespass, Fraud, Negligence & Breach of Contract was filed on July 29, 2016.
Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint was filed on December 2, 2016 and served via U.S. Mail on defendants Scott Nolen and Jason Lalak that same day.
Order permitting service of Summons on First Amended Complaint via publication as to defendant Bryan Chauvel filed April 4, 2018.
Proof of Publication of First Amended Summons & Complaint as to defendant Bryan Chauvel filed May 11, 2018.
Default as to defendant Byran Chauvel was entered June 12, 2018.
Dismissal With Prejudice as to defendants Scott Nolen and Jason Lalak was filed with the Clerk's Office on March 4, 2019.
DOE defendants dismissed October 25, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff 100 Van Ness Avenue, LLC, and against the defaulted defendant Bryan Chauvel, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $15,640.96.
Prejudgment Interest - $10,057.14.
Costs - $450.00.

Total - $26,148.10.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Francisco Torres, Esq. (415-977-0444) of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky LLP for Plaintiff/opposing party 100 Van Ness Associates, LLC.
Aron K. Liang, Esq. (925-225-6838) of Philip M. Andersen & Associates for Defendants/Moving party SCOTT NOLEN and JASON LALAK.

The Court announces Tentative Ruling. Defendants' counsel ARGUED both motions re: Punitive damages and Breach of Contract. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTs SCOTT NOLEN and JASON LALAK's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Causes of action for aiding and abetting, fraud/deceit and breach of contract are sufficiently alleged. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
